Not Sure How to Optimize Claude Code? One Simple Command Points You in the Right Direction!

More and more people are picking up Claude Code these days, and pretty much everyone runs into the same nagging question at some point: "How do I get more out of my Claude Code usage this month?" So this post is about how to maximize what you're getting out of Claude Code, so your dev workflow becomes more efficient.

When you're working with Claude Code, you obviously want it to keep getting smarter so it can help you more precisely — not have to constantly tune your Prompts or constantly tweak Claude Code's settings yourself. That kind of churn gets annoying fast and it's a waste of time.
To deal with this, Claude (and the rest of us) have introduced all kinds of file-management techniques, like…

The general idea is that when you give Claude Code a task, it should be able to find the relevant docs on its own and use them to get the job done — letting you focus on the actual architecture, logic, and feature work.
But sooner or later you hit a wall and run out of ideas. So I want to introduce a really simple built-in command for exactly this situation —
is a built-in command. When you type
into the Claude Code chat box, Claude Code analyzes your usage from the past 28 days and gives you suggestions and directions, so you have a clearer idea of what to optimize next.
It's super easy to use — just type
into the Claude Code chat box and hit Enter.
Then Claude Code analyzes the past 28 days of your conversations and gives you analysis and suggestions, so you have a clearer picture of where to optimize Claude Code next.
Once it's done generating, you can find the path it gives you. Inside, you'll see these files:
: this is the main one to focus on — it contains the analysis and suggestions, giving you a clearer picture of how to optimize your Claude Code.
folder: qualitative analysis results for each session — goals, outcomes, satisfaction, friction points, etc.
folder: quantitative raw data for each session — duration, tool usage counts, Token usage, code change stats, etc.
and you'll see a wall of analysis and suggestions.
The full report is pretty long, but if you want, you only need to focus on these sections:
: analyzes your interaction patterns and habits with Claude Code.
: lists common friction points — like Claude going off in the wrong direction or over-engineering things.
: recommends Claude Code features you haven't used yet but might find useful.
New Ways to Use Claude Code
: gives you Prompt templates you can copy and use directly to improve your workflow.
: more advanced workflow suggestions — things like TDD automation, parallel Agents, etc.
There's also a fun little easter egg at the very bottom of the report.
So to wrap up — here's when you'd want to use
When Claude Code feels less helpful than you expected and you want to figure out where the problem is.
When you're stuck tweaking CLAUDE.md or Prompts and don't know what to optimize next.
When you want to understand your own usage habits and style, and find areas to improve.
When you want to know which Claude Code features you haven't tried yet.
When you've been using it for a while and want to look back at the past 28 days.
Hope this command helps you get a clearer picture of how to optimize your Claude Code!
